## HoneyHive Config Bucket Attributes

Sub-keys of the `config` bucket that identify the model + provider and drive Template-section rendering with `{{variable}}` substitution. All attributes in this group are stable.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-config-model" href="#honeyhive-config-model">`honeyhive.config.model`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Model identifier used for the call. Read by the Playground when loading an event for replay; also used by the Trajectory view as a category label fallback. | `gpt-4o`; `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`; `gemini-1.5-pro` |
| <a id="honeyhive-config-provider" href="#honeyhive-config-provider">`honeyhive.config.provider`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Provider name. Read by the Playground when loading an event for replay; mirrors `metadata.provider` (set from the source `gen_ai.system` attribute or its v1.37+ `gen_ai.provider.name` successor). | `openai`; `anthropic`; `bedrock`; `google` |
| <a id="honeyhive-config-template" href="#honeyhive-config-template">`honeyhive.config.template`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Prompt template. When set as an array of template message objects (each with `role` and `content`), the sideview renders a Template section above the chat history and substitutes `{{variableName}}` placeholders with matching keys from `inputs` (non-chat-history inputs); `template.length` also controls how many leading messages in `inputs.chat_history` are skipped by the main chat renderer. May alternatively be an object with `name`, `version`, and `content` sub-keys. Stored as JSON when transported via OTLP. | `[{"role":"system","content":"You are an assistant. User name: {{user_name}}."}]`; `{"name":"customer_support_v2","version":"3","content":"..."}` |

## HoneyHive Inputs Bucket Attributes

Sub-keys of the `inputs` bucket that drive special platform rendering (Chat History tab, template substitution). All attributes in this group are stable.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-inputs-chat-history" href="#honeyhive-inputs-chat-history">`honeyhive.inputs.chat_history`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Message thread array. Triggers the dedicated Chat History tab in the Input section of the sideview (excluded from the general Inputs key-value view); rendered via OpenAIChatRenderer. When `config.template` is also present, the first `template.length` messages are rendered as template messages with `{{variable}}` substitution. Expected format: array of message objects with at minimum a `role` field and one of `content`, `value`, `tool_calls`, or `function_call`. Stored as JSON when transported via OTLP. | `[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"},{"role":"assistant","content":"Hi there!"}]` |

## HoneyHive Metadata Bucket — Session-Level Aggregates

Session-level aggregate sub-keys of the `metadata` bucket. Set on events where `event_type == "session"` by the writer/normalizer (rolled up from child events); not user-set on non-session events. Source for the Session Summary panel stats and the sessions table columns. All attributes in this group are stable.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-cost" href="#honeyhive-metadata-cost">`honeyhive.metadata.cost`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | double | Aggregate cost in USD attributed to this session (rolled up across child spans by the normalizer). Displayed as the "Cost" stat in the Session Summary panel (formatted as `$X.XXXX`) and as the built-in Cost sizing option in the Trajectory view. | `0.0123`; `1.4521` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-has-feedback" href="#honeyhive-metadata-has-feedback">`honeyhive.metadata.has_feedback`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | boolean | True when the session has at least one feedback annotation across its events. | `true`; `false` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-num-events" href="#honeyhive-metadata-num-events">`honeyhive.metadata.num_events`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | int | Total child event count for the session. Displayed as the "Num of Events" column in the sessions table and the "Number of children" stat in Session Summary. Initialized to 0 by the normalizer on session events. | `12` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-num-model-events" href="#honeyhive-metadata-num-model-events">`honeyhive.metadata.num_model_events`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | int | Model event count (children with `event_type == "model"`). Displayed as the "Num of LLM Requests" column in the sessions table. Initialized to 0 by the normalizer on session events. | `4` |

## HoneyHive Metadata Bucket — Context Identifiers

Context-identifier sub-keys of the `metadata` bucket — user, run, dataset, datapoint, tool-call, and an informational session-id copy. Used for filtering, joining, and dataset-evaluation lineage. All attributes in this group are stable.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-datapoint-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-datapoint-id">`honeyhive.metadata.datapoint_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Datapoint identifier within a dataset. Set when an event is part of a dataset evaluation. | `EXT-5578323c51630e91e001027c` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-dataset-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-dataset-id">`honeyhive.metadata.dataset_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Dataset identifier (HoneyHive dataset). Set when an event is part of a dataset evaluation. | `EXT-2630e2cde725c322d01fee0a` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-run-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-run-id">`honeyhive.metadata.run_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Evaluation run identifier (HoneyHive dataset run). Set when an event is part of an offline experiment / dataset evaluation. | `run_2025_q1_eval_42` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-session-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-session-id">`honeyhive.metadata.session_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | OTel session identifier (informational copy of the source `session.id` attribute). Does NOT override the root `session_id` field that drives session grouping. | `a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef0123456789` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-tool-call-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-tool-call-id">`honeyhive.metadata.tool_call_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Tool-call identifier. Present on tool-result spans; used to correlate the tool result back to the originating tool call in chat history. Mapped from `gen_ai.tool.call.id` across OTel GenAI, OpenInference, and Traceloop. | `call_abc123` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-user-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-user-id">`honeyhive.metadata.user_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | User identifier. Mapped from the OpenInference `user.id` source attribute. Used for per-user filtering and aggregation. | `user_8f3c1d29` |

## HoneyHive Metadata Bucket — Finish Reasons

Finish-reason sub-keys of the `metadata` bucket. The scalar `finish_reason` is extracted as the first element of the source `gen_ai.response.finish_reasons` array; the full array is preserved in `finish_reasons` for multi-choice responses. Mixed stability: `finish_reason` and `finish_reasons` are stable; the `response_finish_reasons` OpenInference alias is development-tier.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-finish-reason" href="#honeyhive-metadata-finish-reason">`honeyhive.metadata.finish_reason`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Scalar model finish reason. Extracted as the first element of the source `gen_ai.response.finish_reasons` array (OTel GenAI, Traceloop) or set directly from `gen_ai.response.finish_reason` (OpenInference singular form). | `stop`; `length`; `tool_calls`; `content_filter` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-finish-reasons" href="#honeyhive-metadata-finish-reasons">`honeyhive.metadata.finish_reasons`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string[] | Array of all model finish reasons (one entry per response choice). Mapped from `gen_ai.response.finish_reasons` across OTel GenAI, OpenInference, and Traceloop. | `["stop"]`; `["stop", "tool_calls"]` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-response-finish-reasons" href="#honeyhive-metadata-response-finish-reasons">`honeyhive.metadata.response_finish_reasons`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string[] | Array of model finish reasons set by the OpenInference ingestion strategy alongside `metadata.finish_reasons` for backwards compatibility with older OpenInference versions that emitted the attribute under a `response_*` prefix. Both keys are stored when the source attribute is present. [1] | `["stop"]`; `["length", "tool_calls"]` |

**[1] `honeyhive.metadata.response_finish_reasons`:** Prefer `metadata.finish_reasons` (stable) for new code paths. This alias may be removed in a future minor release once OpenInference instrumentation has aged out.

## HoneyHive Metadata Bucket — Identity Attributes

Agent and model identity sub-keys of the `metadata` bucket. Drive the Trajectory view category labels and the resolved-model display in the sideview. Mixed stability: the explicitly-stable sub-keys (`agent_name`, `agent_id`, `tool_name`, `model_name`, `provider`) are populated by the ingestion normalizer from OTel GenAI, OpenInference, and Traceloop; development-tier complements cover span classification, alias keys, and informational copies of root event fields.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-agent-description" href="#honeyhive-metadata-agent-description">`honeyhive.metadata.agent_description`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Free-text description of the agent that produced the span. | `A helpful research assistant that can analyze information and provide insights`; `An agent that provides assistance with ability to use tools.` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-agent-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-agent-id">`honeyhive.metadata.agent_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Agent identifier. Mapped from `gen_ai.agent.id` across OTel GenAI, OpenInference, and Traceloop. | `agent-7c1f9d34` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-agent-name" href="#honeyhive-metadata-agent-name">`honeyhive.metadata.agent_name`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Agent name. Read by the Trajectory view as the primary per-row category label (first non-empty wins, falling back to `tool_name`, `config.model`, then `event_name`). | `planning_agent`; `router` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-conversation-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-conversation-id">`honeyhive.metadata.conversation_id`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Provider-side conversation identifier, normalized from `gen_ai.conversation.id` (OTel) or `session.id` (OpenInference). [2] | `conv-7f3a9c` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-event-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-event-id">`honeyhive.metadata.event_id`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Internal copy of an upstream event identifier — typically the MCP/tool-call ID or a Google ADK event ID — set into metadata alongside the root `event_id`. [3] | `tool_call_a1b2c3` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-event-name" href="#honeyhive-metadata-event-name">`honeyhive.metadata.event_name`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Informational copy of the event name label set by the ingestion normalizer alongside the root `event_name`. Does not override the root field. | `openai.chat.completion`; `retrieve_documents` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-event-type" href="#honeyhive-metadata-event-type">`honeyhive.metadata.event_type`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Informational copy of the event type label set by the ingestion normalizer alongside the root `event_type`. Does not override the root field. | `model`; `tool`; `agent` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-llm-model-name" href="#honeyhive-metadata-llm-model-name">`honeyhive.metadata.llm.model_name`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Legacy dotted alias for the resolved model name, set by older OpenInference instrumentation alongside `metadata.model_name`. Both keys are stored when the source attribute is present. [4] | `gpt-4o-mini`; `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-model-name" href="#honeyhive-metadata-model-name">`honeyhive.metadata.model_name`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Resolved model name (response model when available, otherwise request model). The normalizer falls back to `config.model` when no response model is reported. Use `metadata.response_model` for the exact model returned by the API. | `gpt-4o-2024-08-06`; `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-operation-name" href="#honeyhive-metadata-operation-name">`honeyhive.metadata.operation_name`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | GenAI operation name (`chat`, `completion`, `embedding`, `execute_tool`, …). | `chat`; `execute_tool`; `embedding` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-provider" href="#honeyhive-metadata-provider">`honeyhive.metadata.provider`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Provider name (mirrors `config.provider`). Set from the source `gen_ai.provider.name` (OTel v1.37+) or `llm.provider` (OpenInference, Traceloop). Distinct from `metadata.system`, which mirrors `gen_ai.system`. | `openai`; `anthropic` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-request-type" href="#honeyhive-metadata-request-type">`honeyhive.metadata.request_type`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | LLM request type (`chat`, `completion`, `embedding`, …). | `chat`; `completion`; `embedding` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-span-kind" href="#honeyhive-metadata-span-kind">`honeyhive.metadata.span_kind`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Instrumentor span classification, normalized from `openinference.span.kind`, `traceloop.span.kind`, and (in OTel v1.37+) `gen_ai.agent.type`. Used as a fallback signal when mapping a span to the HoneyHive root `event_type`. [5] | `LLM`; `AGENT`; `TOOL` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-system" href="#honeyhive-metadata-system">`honeyhive.metadata.system`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Provider system string mirroring `config.provider` (stable); set by the normalizer from `gen_ai.system`. [6] | `openai`; `anthropic`; `bedrock` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-tool-name" href="#honeyhive-metadata-tool-name">`honeyhive.metadata.tool_name`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Tool name. Read by the Trajectory view as a category label fallback after `agent_name`. Mapped from `gen_ai.tool.name` across OTel GenAI, OpenInference, and Traceloop. | `web_search`; `sql_query_tool` |

**[2] `honeyhive.metadata.conversation_id`:** Does not override the HoneyHive root `session_id` field. `metadata.conversation_id` records the upstream concept; root `session_id` is the HoneyHive grouping key.

**[3] `honeyhive.metadata.event_id`:** Informational only. Does not override the root `event_id` field used to key the event in storage.

**[4] `honeyhive.metadata.llm.model_name`:** Prefer `metadata.model_name` (stable, HHAI-5104) for new code. This alias persists so historical events instrumented with older OpenInference versions remain queryable by the same key they were written with.

**[5] `honeyhive.metadata.span_kind`:** Values are stored in the casing emitted by the source instrumentor (OpenInference uppercases `LLM`/`TOOL`/`AGENT`; Traceloop lowercases `llm`/`tool`/`agent`). The enum captures the OpenInference casing as canonical members; extra string values are accepted.

**[6] `honeyhive.metadata.system`:** `metadata.system` and `config.provider` are populated from different source attributes. `gen_ai.system` (all three ecosystems) flows to `metadata.system`. `gen_ai.provider.name` (OTel v1.37+) and `llm.provider` (OpenInference, Traceloop) flow to `config.provider`.

---

`honeyhive.metadata.span_kind` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value | Description | Stability |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `AGENT` | Agent step span | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `CHAIN` | Chain or workflow span | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `EMBEDDING` | Embedding span | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `LLM` | LLM completion span | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `RETRIEVER` | Retrieval span | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `TASK` | Traceloop task span | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `TOOL` | Tool / function-call span | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |
| `WORKFLOW` | Traceloop workflow span | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) |

## HoneyHive Metadata Bucket — Latency

Latency sub-keys of the `metadata` bucket. Both attributes in this group are stable.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-latency-ms" href="#honeyhive-metadata-latency-ms">`honeyhive.metadata.latency_ms`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | double | End-to-end request latency in milliseconds. Distinct from the root `duration` field (which is wall-clock for the entire span); `latency_ms` reflects the underlying API call latency reported by the instrumentation. | `1234.5` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-time-to-first-token-ms" href="#honeyhive-metadata-time-to-first-token-ms">`honeyhive.metadata.time_to_first_token_ms`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | double | Time to first streaming token in milliseconds. Reported by instrumentations that observe streamed responses. | `187.3` |

## HoneyHive MCP Attributes

Canonical honeyhive.metadata.mcp_* attributes set by the ingestion normalizer when the source span is an MCP client/server invocation. Render in the Metadata sideview section. Every attribute in this group is development tier.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-mcp-client-name" href="#honeyhive-metadata-mcp-client-name">`honeyhive.metadata.mcp_client_name`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | MCP client name attached to the span by the MCP-aware instrumentation. [7] | `honeyhive-client`; `cursor` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-mcp-request-type" href="#honeyhive-metadata-mcp-request-type">`honeyhive.metadata.mcp_request_type`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | MCP request kind (e.g., `tool_call`, `list_tools`, `read_resource`). | `tool_call`; `list_tools`; `read_resource` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-mcp-server-name" href="#honeyhive-metadata-mcp-server-name">`honeyhive.metadata.mcp_server_name`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | MCP server name attached to the span by the MCP-aware instrumentation. [8] | `filesystem`; `github` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-mcp-session-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-mcp-session-id">`honeyhive.metadata.mcp_session_id`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | MCP-protocol session identifier; distinct from the HoneyHive root `session_id`. [9] | `mcp-sess-7f3a9c` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-mcp-tool-name" href="#honeyhive-metadata-mcp-tool-name">`honeyhive.metadata.mcp_tool_name`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | MCP tool name being invoked on the MCP server. [10] | `read_file`; `list_repositories` |

**[7] `honeyhive.metadata.mcp_client_name`:** Routed into the metadata bucket. No corresponding stable attribute exists in OTel GenAI v1.36 or v1.37; HoneyHive normalizes from the OpenInference and standard-genai source attributes that carry the client name on the MCP-tool-call span.

**[8] `honeyhive.metadata.mcp_server_name`:** Routed into the metadata bucket. No stable cross-ecosystem source.

**[9] `honeyhive.metadata.mcp_session_id`:** Does not override the root `session_id` field used for trace grouping. Stored alongside it for protocol-level diagnostics.

**[10] `honeyhive.metadata.mcp_tool_name`:** Distinct from `config.tool_name` and `metadata.tool_name` — those carry the framework-level tool identity. `mcp_tool_name` carries the tool identifier as exposed by the MCP server.

## HoneyHive Multimodal Attributes

Development-tier metadata.* MIME-type attributes describing the input and output modalities of multimodal spans (vision, audio, embedded files). Render in the Metadata sideview section.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-input-mime-type" href="#honeyhive-metadata-input-mime-type">`honeyhive.metadata.input_mime_type`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | MIME type of the input payload. | `text/plain`; `image/jpeg`; `application/json` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-output-mime-type" href="#honeyhive-metadata-output-mime-type">`honeyhive.metadata.output_mime_type`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | MIME type of the output payload. | `text/plain`; `application/json` |

## HoneyHive Observability Edge Attributes

Development-tier metadata.* attributes for tool execution status, messaging-system context, and ingestion-time HTTP-span suppression. Render in the Metadata sideview section.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-disable-http-tracing" href="#honeyhive-metadata-disable-http-tracing">`honeyhive.metadata.disable_http_tracing`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | boolean | When true, the HoneyHive ingestion pipeline suppresses HTTP spans emitted by the same trace. Set by the SDK on the root span when the application opts out of low-level HTTP tracing to reduce noise. [11] | `true`; `false` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-messaging-destination" href="#honeyhive-metadata-messaging-destination">`honeyhive.metadata.messaging_destination`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Messaging-system destination topic or queue, set on spans emitted by messaging-aware instrumentations (e.g., Semantic Kernel agent runtime). Mirrors `config.destination` (stable). [12] | `Writer_dff60b366ebe4fe4b2312019d5ae816c.(default)-A`; `agent-inbox` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-tool-status" href="#honeyhive-metadata-tool-status">`honeyhive.metadata.tool_status`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Tool execution status string set by tool/agent instrumentations. | `success`; `error` |

**[11] `honeyhive.metadata.disable_http_tracing`:** Default behavior in the HoneyHive Python SDK is to suppress HTTP spans (`disable_http_tracing=True`). Set this attribute explicitly only when overriding the SDK default, or when emitting via REST/OTLP without going through the SDK.

**[12] `honeyhive.metadata.messaging_destination`:** The Traceloop strategy splits the source attribute across two buckets: `config.destination` for runtime configuration and `metadata.messaging_destination` for the operational record.

## HoneyHive Metadata Bucket — OTLP Traceability

OTLP traceability sub-keys of the `metadata` bucket. Set on every OTLP-ingested event by the ingestion pipeline; preserved verbatim from the source OTel span. Used by the tree-builder to reconstruct parent-child relationships via span ID lineage instead of temporal containment, and by support to correlate ingested events back to the originating OTLP payload. All attributes in this group are stable.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-has-otlp-lineage" href="#honeyhive-metadata-has-otlp-lineage">`honeyhive.metadata.has_otlp_lineage`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | boolean | Always `true` on OTLP-ingested events. Signals the tree-builder to use span ID lineage instead of temporal containment when establishing parent-child relationships. | `true` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-honeyhive-event-type" href="#honeyhive-metadata-honeyhive-event-type">`honeyhive.metadata.honeyhive_event_type`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Preserved copy of the `honeyhive_event_type` source attribute when present on the OTLP span (informational; does not override the root `event_type` field). | `session`; `model`; `tool` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-instrumentor" href="#honeyhive-metadata-instrumentor">`honeyhive.metadata.instrumentor`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Detected instrumentation library that emitted the OTLP span. Drives the strategy-routing logic in the ingestion normalizer (which set of source-attribute mappings to apply). | `openinference`; `traceloop`; `aws-strands`; `google-adk`; `standardgenai` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-parent-span-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-parent-span-id">`honeyhive.metadata.parent_span_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Original OTel parent span identifier (hex string). Used by the tree-builder to reconstruct trace tree lineage before the root `parent_id` field is resolved. Empty on root spans. | `00f067aa0ba902b7` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-span-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-span-id">`honeyhive.metadata.span_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Original OTel span identifier (hex string). Preserved from the source OTLP span on every OTLP-ingested event. | `00f067aa0ba902b7` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-trace-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-trace-id">`honeyhive.metadata.trace_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Original OTel trace identifier (hex string). Preserved from the source OTLP span on every OTLP-ingested event. | `4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736` |

## HoneyHive Response Context Attributes

Development-tier metadata.* attributes describing the underlying provider API response. Useful for replay, debugging, and provider diagnostics. Render in the Metadata sideview section.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-openai-api-base" href="#honeyhive-metadata-openai-api-base">`honeyhive.metadata.openai_api_base`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | OpenAI-compatible API base URL the request was issued against. Useful when traffic flows through a proxy, gateway, or self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoint. | `https://api.openai.com/v1`; `https://my-llm-gateway.internal/v1` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-openai-system-fingerprint" href="#honeyhive-metadata-openai-system-fingerprint">`honeyhive.metadata.openai_system_fingerprint`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | OpenAI system fingerprint from the response, identifying the backend serving configuration that produced the completion. | `fp_abc123def4` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-response-id" href="#honeyhive-metadata-response-id">`honeyhive.metadata.response_id`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Provider response identifier (e.g., OpenAI `response.id`, Anthropic message id). | `chatcmpl-9abc123`; `msg_01a2b3c4d5` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-response-model" href="#honeyhive-metadata-response-model">`honeyhive.metadata.response_model`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | string | Resolved model name returned by the provider API. May differ from the request model (`config.model`) when the provider aliases or upgrades the requested model. [13] | `gpt-4o-2024-08-06`; `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-status-code" href="#honeyhive-metadata-status-code">`honeyhive.metadata.status_code`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | int | HTTP status code returned by the underlying provider API call. [14] | `200`; `429`; `500` |

**[13] `honeyhive.metadata.response_model`:** `metadata.model_name` (stable, HHAI-5104) is the resolved model name shown in the UI; the normalizer prefers `response_model` when available and falls back to `config.model` otherwise. Use `metadata.response_model` directly when you need the exact API response model regardless of fallback.

**[14] `honeyhive.metadata.status_code`:** Distinct from the root `error` field. `status_code` records the transport-layer response; `error` records the SDK-level exception (or null on success).

## HoneyHive Development Token Counts

Development-tier metadata.* token-count attributes for prompt caching and reasoning models. Used by Anthropic prompt cache, OpenAI o-series, and Claude extended-thinking spans. Render in the Metadata sideview section.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-cache-read-input-tokens" href="#honeyhive-metadata-cache-read-input-tokens">`honeyhive.metadata.cache_read_input_tokens`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | int | Number of input tokens served from the provider prompt cache (Anthropic, OpenAI). | `1024`; `0` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-cache-write-input-tokens" href="#honeyhive-metadata-cache-write-input-tokens">`honeyhive.metadata.cache_write_input_tokens`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | int | Number of input tokens written to the provider prompt cache (Anthropic, Traceloop OTel). [15] | `512`; `0` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-reasoning-tokens" href="#honeyhive-metadata-reasoning-tokens">`honeyhive.metadata.reasoning_tokens`</a> | ![Development](https://img.shields.io/badge/-development-blue) | int | Internal reasoning tokens consumed by the model (OpenAI o-series, Claude extended thinking). These tokens are billed but not surfaced in the assistant message. | `256`; `0` |

**[15] `honeyhive.metadata.cache_write_input_tokens`:** Documented in the framework attribute mapping table but absent from the canonical metadata sub-key list in PR #273. Treated as a development gap to be reconciled when promoting prompt-cache accounting to stable.

## HoneyHive Metadata Bucket — Token Counts

Token-count sub-keys of the `metadata` bucket. Aggregated into session-level totals by the writer's session aggregator and stored in `session_aggregates`; the Session Summary "Total Tokens" stat reads the aggregated value. All attributes in this group are stable.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-completion-tokens" href="#honeyhive-metadata-completion-tokens">`honeyhive.metadata.completion_tokens`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | int | Output/completion token count (legacy alias for `output_tokens`). Mapped from `gen_ai.usage.completion_tokens` and `llm.token_count.completion`. Stored alongside `output_tokens` when both source attributes are present. | `256` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-input-tokens" href="#honeyhive-metadata-input-tokens">`honeyhive.metadata.input_tokens`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | int | Input/prompt token count (OTel v1.36+ canonical name). Mapped from `gen_ai.usage.input_tokens`. Preferred over `prompt_tokens` in new instrumentation; stored alongside it when both source attributes are present. | `512` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-output-tokens" href="#honeyhive-metadata-output-tokens">`honeyhive.metadata.output_tokens`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | int | Output/completion token count (OTel v1.36+ canonical name). Mapped from `gen_ai.usage.output_tokens`. Preferred over `completion_tokens` in new instrumentation; stored alongside it when both source attributes are present. | `256` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-prompt-tokens" href="#honeyhive-metadata-prompt-tokens">`honeyhive.metadata.prompt_tokens`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | int | Input/prompt token count (legacy alias for `input_tokens`). Mapped from `gen_ai.usage.prompt_tokens` and `llm.token_count.prompt`. Stored alongside `input_tokens` when both source attributes are present. | `512` |
| <a id="honeyhive-metadata-total-tokens" href="#honeyhive-metadata-total-tokens">`honeyhive.metadata.total_tokens`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | int | Total token count. Auto-computed by the normalizer from `prompt_tokens + completion_tokens` (or `input_tokens + output_tokens`) when not explicitly set. Source for the "Total Tokens" stat in Session Summary. | `768` |

## HoneyHive Outputs Bucket Attributes

Sub-keys of the `outputs` bucket that select the chat-message rendering mode in the sideview, reconstruct flattened tool-call structures, and bypass bucket rendering for plain-text outputs. All attributes in this group are stable.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-outputs-chat-history" href="#honeyhive-outputs-chat-history">`honeyhive.outputs.chat_history`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Array of output messages. When `outputs.role` is absent, the presence of this key still triggers chat-message rendering mode and the first element is rendered as the primary output. Stored as JSON when transported via OTLP; expected format matches `inputs.chat_history`. | `[{"role":"assistant","content":"The answer is 42."}]` |
| <a id="honeyhive-outputs-role" href="#honeyhive-outputs-role">`honeyhive.outputs.role`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Speaker role for the output message. Presence of this key triggers the chat-message rendering mode in the sideview (capitalised as the speaker label). When absent, the output renders in the generic markdown/JSON toggle view. | `assistant`; `tool` |
| <a id="honeyhive-outputs-text" href="#honeyhive-outputs-text">`honeyhive.outputs.text`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Plain-text output. When set, this string is used as the output value instead of the full `outputs` object; rendered in the standard markdown/JSON toggle view. | `The weather in San Francisco is 68F and sunny.` |
| <a id="honeyhive-outputs-tool-calls" href="#honeyhive-outputs-tool-calls">`honeyhive.outputs.tool_calls`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Array of tool-call objects (each with `id`, `function.name`, `function.arguments`). When set as flattened dot-notation keys (`tool_calls.0.id`, `tool_calls.0.function.name`, ...), the sideview reconstructs a proper `tool_calls` array before rendering. Each tool call is rendered as a named JSON block. Stored as JSON when transported via OTLP. | `[{"id":"call_abc","function":{"name":"get_weather","arguments":"{\"city\":\"SF\"}"}}]` |

## HoneyHive Root Span Attributes

Top-level fields stored directly on every HoneyHive event. Drive trace tree rendering, sideview headers, timeline positioning, and error indicators. All attributes in this group are stable and safe to reference in evaluator configuration, dashboards, and saved filters.

**Attributes:**

| Key | Stability | Value Type | Description | Example Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="honeyhive-duration" href="#honeyhive-duration">`honeyhive.duration`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | double | Wall-clock duration in milliseconds. Displayed as the "Latency" column in the events table and as the duration badge on each trace tree node. Computed automatically from `end_time - start_time` if not set explicitly. | `1234.5` |
| <a id="honeyhive-error" href="#honeyhive-error">`honeyhive.error`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Error message captured if the traced function raised. When non-null and non-empty, renders a red error panel at the top of the Output section and flips the trace-tree node from green checkmark to red X. The error string is JSON-stringified before display. | `Connection timed out after 30s`; `Tool input failed schema validation: missing field "user_id"` |
| <a id="honeyhive-event-name" href="#honeyhive-event-name">`honeyhive.event_name`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Human-readable span title shown in the trace tree, sideview header, timeline, and trajectory view (as a fallback label when no agent / tool / model name is found). | `openai.chat.completion`; `retrieve_documents`; `agent.plan_step` |
| <a id="honeyhive-event-type" href="#honeyhive-event-type">`honeyhive.event_type`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Event classification. Drives icon, color coding, and available sideview affordances. Trajectory view filters out `session` (and legacy `conversation`) values when grouping non-session events. | `session`; `model`; `tool` |
| <a id="honeyhive-parent-id" href="#honeyhive-parent-id">`honeyhive.parent_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Parent span identifier. Used to build the trace tree and to determine sibling relationships (controls prev/next navigation between sibling spans in the sideview). | `a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef0123456789` |
| <a id="honeyhive-session-id" href="#honeyhive-session-id">`honeyhive.session_id`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | string | Session grouping identifier. Links every span emitted within one end-to-end invocation; routes ingested events into the same session tree, timeline, graph, and trajectory views. | `a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef0123456789` |
| <a id="honeyhive-start-time" href="#honeyhive-start-time">`honeyhive.start_time`</a> | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) | int | Span start timestamp in Unix milliseconds. Children within a session are sorted by `start_time` ascending before trace tree rendering; also displayed as the event timestamp in the table and sideview header, and used by the trajectory view to build the left-to-right step sequence. | `1714492800123` |

---

`honeyhive.event_type` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value | Description | Stability |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chain` | Generic chain or sub-step span. Standard sideview layout. | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `model` | LLM completion span. Renders the "Open in Playground" sideview button. | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `session` | Top-level session/conversation span. Enables Session Summary panel and Composite Evaluations section. | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
| `tool` | Tool / function-call span. Standard sideview layout. | ![Stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/-stable-lightgreen) |
